Class ControlPanel

java.lang.Object
net.fokinatorr.sparkle.stage.ControlPanel
All Implemented Interfaces:
Renderable, Renderable2D
public class ControlPanel extends Object implements Renderable2D
A stage panel that contains control buttons. Pressing these buttons may change the stage's state (e.g. start or stop its loop).

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static enum 
    ControlPanel.Button
    Represents a button controlling state of a stage.
    static enum 
    ControlPanel.Corner
    Represents a stage's corner to anchor a control panel to.

    • ControlPanel

      public ControlPanel()

  • Method Details

    • register

      public static ControlPanel register(Stage stage, String id, ControlPanel.Corner corner)
      Creates a control panel and registers it in the given stage under specified ID anchored at the given corner.
      Parameters:
      stage - the stage to attach to
      id - the unique identifier of this panel in the stage
      corner - the corner to anchor to
      Returns:
      a new control panel

    • enableButton

      public void enableButton(ControlPanel.Button button)
      Enables the given control button in this panel.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the button is null

    • disableButton

      public void disableButton(ControlPanel.Button button)
      Disables the given control button in this panel.

    • getRotation

      public double getRotation(RenderingContext ctx)
      Description copied from interface: Renderable2D
      Returns the rotation in degrees for this object.
      Specified by:
      getRotation in interface Renderable2D
      Parameters:
      ctx - the rendering context
      Returns:
      the rotation in degrees for this object

    • getBounds

      public Hitbox2D getBounds(RenderingContext ctx)
      Description copied from interface: Renderable2D
      Returns this object's on-screen bounding hitbox, as if without any transformations applied. This can be used to check what object was clicked, what objects intersect, etc.

      Note: This method should not modify the given context in any way to avoid sudden side effects.

      Specified by:
      getBounds in interface Renderable2D
      Parameters:
      ctx - the rendering context
      Returns:
      the bounding box

    • getScreenScaleX

      public double getScreenScaleX(RenderingContext ctx)
      Description copied from interface: Renderable2D
      Returns the X scale coefficient for this object.
      Specified by:
      getScreenScaleX in interface Renderable2D
      Parameters:
      ctx - the rendering context
      Returns:
      the X scale coefficient for this object

      Returns:

    • render

      public void render(RenderCanvas canvas)
      Description copied from interface: Renderable2D
      Render this object onto the given canvas. The canvas's size will not be more than values returned by Renderable.getPreferredCanvasSize(RenderingContext). The object must be rendered as if without any transformations applied.
      Specified by:
      render in interface Renderable
      Specified by:
      render in interface Renderable2D
      Parameters:
      canvas - the canvas

    • getPreferredCanvasSize

      public Point2D getPreferredCanvasSize(RenderingContext ctx)
      Description copied from interface: Renderable
      Returns preferred dimensions of the object's render. If a dimension exceeds the canvas's dimension, the latter one is used instead.

      Note: This method should not modify the given context in any way to avoid sudden side effects.

      Specified by:
      getPreferredCanvasSize in interface Renderable
      Parameters:
      ctx - the rendering context
      Returns:
      the preferred dimensions
